title: Anthony Albanese becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Anthony Norman Albanese is an Australian politician who has served as the 31st prime minister of Australia since 2022.

title: John Howard becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: John Winston Howard is an Australian former politician who served as the 25th prime minister of Australia from 1996 to 2007, holding office as leader of the Liberal Party of Australia.

title: Harold Holt becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Harold Edward Holt was an Australian politician who served as the 17th prime minister of Australia from 1966 until his disappearance and presumed death in 1967.

title: Tony Abbott becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Anthony John Abbott is an Australian former politician who served as the 28th prime minister of Australia from 2013 to 2015.

title: Scott Morrison becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Scott John Morrison is an Australian former politician who served as the 30th prime minister of Australia and the leader of the Liberal Party from 2018 to 2022.

title: Julia Gillard becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Julia Eileen Gillard is an Australian former politician who served as the 27th prime minister of Australia from 2010 to 2013.

title: Gough Whitlam becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Edward Gough Whitlam was the 21st prime minister of Australia, serving from December 1972 to November 1975.

title: Kevin Rudd becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Kevin Michael Rudd is an Australian diplomat and former politician who served as the 26th prime minister of Australia from 2007 to 2010 and in 2013.

title: Malcolm Turnbull becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Malcolm Bligh Turnbull is an Australian former politician, businessman and barrister who served as the 29th prime minister of Australia from 2015 to 2018.

title: Bob Hawke becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Robert James Lee Hawke was an Australian politician and trade unionist who served as the 23rd prime minister of Australia from 1983 to 1991.

title: Paul Keating becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Paul John Keating is an Australian former politician who served as the 24th prime minister of Australia from 1991 to 1996.

title: Robert Menzies becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Sir Robert Gordon Menzies was an Australian politician and lawyer who served as the 12th prime minister of Australia from 1939 to 1941 and from 1949 to 1966.

title: William McMahon becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Sir William McMahon was an Australian politician who served as the 20th prime minister of Australia from 1971 to 1972.

title: John Curtin becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: John Curtin was an Australian politician who served as the 14th prime minister of Australia from 1941 until his death in 1945.

title: Malcolm Fraser becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: John Malcolm Fraser was an Australian farmer and politician who was the 22nd prime minister of Australia, serving from 1975 to 1983.

title: Edmund Barton becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Sir Edmund "Toby" Barton was an Australian politician, barrister and jurist who served as the first prime minister of Australia from 1901 to 1903.

title: John Gorton becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Sir John Grey Gorton was an Australian politician, farmer and airman who served as the 19th prime minister of Australia from 1968 to 1971.

title: Alfred Deakin becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Alfred Deakin was an Australian politician who served as the second prime minister of Australia from 1903 to 1904, 1905 to 1908, and 1909 to 1910.

title: Billy Hughes becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: William Morris Hughes was an Australian politician who served as the seventh prime minister of Australia from 1915 to 1923.

title: John McEwen becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Sir John McEwen was an Australian politician and farmer who served as the 18th prime minister of Australia from 1967 to 1968, in a caretaker capacity following the disappearance of prime minister Harold Holt.

title: Ben Chifley becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Joseph Benedict Chifley was an Australian politician who served as the 16th prime minister of Australia from 1945 to 1949.

title: Joseph Lyons becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Joseph Aloysius Lyons was an Australian politician who served as the tenth prime minister of Australia, from 1932 until his death in 1939.

title: Andrew Fisher becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Andrew Fisher was an Australian politician and trade unionist who served as the fifth prime minister of Australia from 1908 to 1909, 1910 to 1913 and 1914 to 1915.

title: Frank Forde becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Francis Michael Forde was an Australian politician who served as the 15th prime minister of Australia from 6 to 13 July 1945, in a caretaker capacity following the death of John Curtin.

title: Chris Watson becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: John Christian Watson was an Australian politician who served as the third prime minister of Australia from April to August 1904.

title: S. M. Bruce becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Stanley Melbourne Bruce, 1st Viscount Bruce of Melbourne was an Australian politician, statesman and businessman who served as the eighth prime minister of Australia from 1923 to 1929.

title: Arthur Fadden becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Sir Arthur William Fadden was an Australian politician and accountant who served as the 13th prime minister of Australia from 29 August to 7 October 1941.

title: Earle Page becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Sir Earle Christmas Grafton Page was an Australian politician and surgeon who served as the 11th Prime Minister of Australia from 7 to 26 April 1939, in a caretaker capacity following the death of Joseph Lyons.

title: James Scullin becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: James Henry Scullin was an Australian politician and trade unionist who served as the ninth prime minister of Australia from 1929 to 1932.

title: Joseph Cook becomes prime minister of Australia
fact: Sir Joseph Cook was an Australian politician and trade unionist who served as the sixth prime minister of Australia from 1913 to 1914.
